name: Fresh and Smoked Salmon Pate
recipeIngredient: 1 pound Fresh salmon fillets with skin
Plus a few sprigs for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Whisk together heavy cream and sour cream in a small bowl. Refrigerate.
2. Combine cold water, carrot, onion, bay leaf, lemon slices, and chili pepper in a large skillet that can hold the salmon fillets in one layer. Bring to boiling; continue to boil for 10 minutes. Lower to simmer.
3. Add salmon, skin side up. Simmer very gently over medium-low heat until salmon is just cooked, about 10 minutes. Do not overcook. Remove salmon from poaching liquid and discard the skin. Cool completely.
4. Heat 1 1/2 tablespoons butter in a small skillet over medium heat. Saute shallots until golden, about 5 minutes. Transfer to a bowl of food processor.
5. Add salmon, smoked salmon, and cream mixture to the food processor. Puree until smooth. With the processor running, add the remaining 1/2 cup butter, bit by bit, until smooth.
6. Add lemon juice, salt, pepper, and dill. Fold in until combined.
7. Spoon the mixture into a 4-cup soufflé dish or decorative serving dish. Smooth the top with a rubber spatula.
8. Garnish with a few fresh dill sprigs, a whole bay leaf, and small strips of sweet red pepper, if desired.
9. Pour clarified butter over the pate to cover completely. Refrigerate for 4 hours to set the pate.
